data.q: What is the primary goal when migrating legacy PHP code to a modern codebase?
o: Improve code quality and maintainability without breaking functionality
data.e: Legacy migration aims to modernize the code with better quality and maintainability while preserving its existing behavior.

data.q: Which PHP feature is recommended to replace the deprecated 'mysql_*' functions during legacy migration?
data.e: Modern PHP uses mysqli or PDO for secure and robust database access; the old mysql_* functions are deprecated.

data.q: Which tool can be used to analyze and fix legacy PHP code to modern standards automatically?
data.e: PHPStan (static analysis), PHP CodeSniffer (coding standard checks), and PHP-CS-Fixer (automatic code fixes) help modernize legacy PHP.

data.q: Why is dependency injection preferred over using global variables or singletons in modern PHP applications?
o: It makes the code less testable
It improves code modularity and testability
data.e: Dependency injection reduces coupling and makes unit testing easier by explicitly providing dependencies.

data.q: What modern PHP feature should replace the legacy array() syntax for better readability?
data.e: Since PHP 5.4, the short array syntax [] is preferred over array() for cleaner code.

data.q: When migrating legacy code, why is it important to use namespaces?
o: To allow multiple classes with the same name and organize code logically
data.e: Namespaces help prevent name conflicts and logically group related classes and functions in modern PHP.

data.q: How can legacy spaghetti code be improved during PHP migration?
o: By refactoring into smaller, single-responsibility classes and methods
data.e: Refactoring into well-defined classes/methods improves readability, maintainability, and testability.

data.q: Which is a recommended approach to test legacy PHP code before and after migration?
o: Write automated unit and integration tests
data.e: Automated tests help ensure legacy functionality is preserved while modernizing the code.

data.q: What is one significant benefit of using Composer during legacy PHP migration?
o: It manages class autoloading and external dependencies efficiently
It removes the need for namespaces
data.e: Composer handles dependency management and class autoloading, simplifying modern PHP project structure.

data.q: How should error handling be modernized in PHP legacy migration?
o: Stick with custom error codes in return values
Use PHP exceptions with try-catch blocks
data.e: Using exceptions provides a consistent and robust method for error handling in modern PHP.
